title: CausalGraph
description: The runtime truth (rule R2). The DAG runtime ONLY traverses edges with approved=true, within each edge's lag window. Must be acyclic (validated at compile via topological sort). No AI bypasses this; AI may only PROPOSE edges that a human approves offline.

id.description: Node id. For asset nodes this equals an asset_id; for alarm/situation nodes it is the alarm/situation id.

kind.description: What this node represents in the causal model.

evidence_tags.description: Tags whose values form this node's evidence fingerprint during traversal.

edge_type.description: Physical nature of the causal link.

approved.description: ONLY approved edges enter the runtime traversal. Unapproved edges are visible in Studio for review but never used live.

lag_ms.description: [lag_min_ms, lag_max_ms]. Time-respecting traversal only follows from→to if the effect occurred within this window after the cause. min <= max (validated).

confidence.description: How confident the authoring evidence is. Engineer-entered edges = 1.0.

provenance.description: Where this edge came from. agent_proposed edges MUST have approved=false until a human approves them.

root_cause_rules.description: Optional declarative fingerprints that mark a node as a likely root given a tag pattern. Keep human-readable.

target_node.description: Node this rule scores as a root candidate.

logic.description: Human-readable boolean expression over evidence, e.g. 'motor_vibration_high && bus_voltage_normal'. The engine evaluates a parsed/whitelisted form, not eval().
